explanation: This command uses cmd.exe to perform an MX record lookup for example.com, which is a common reconnaissance technique. The Sigma rule focuses on 'nslookup' with a specific target pattern, but this command bypasses it by using a different command-line tool (cmd.exe) to achieve the same goal.

This PowerShell command performs an MX record lookup for example.com using PowerShell's built-in DNS resolution capabilities. It bypasses the Sigma rule by using PowerShell instead of the 'nslookup' command, which is the primary focus of the rule.

This PowerShell command retrieves MX records for example.com from a DNS server. It bypasses the Sigma rule by using PowerShell's DNS resolution functions instead of the 'nslookup' command, which is the specific focus of the rule.
The command uses nslookup but does not contain the LDAP SRV query string '_ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.', so it bypasses the rule that requires both substrings.
